priority_basis: M-001 is the only canonical continuity-owned item; its handoff requires contradiction modeling without overwriting history, and the current cross-root CM-0010 collision blocks aggregate validation.

completed.action: Migrated the later peer-trigger layering memory from duplicate ID CM-0010 to canonical ID CM-0013 while retaining legacy identity and pre-migration provenance.
completed.memory_rule: Continuity IDs are repository-wide semantic keys across sanctioned storage roots.

claim_states.observed: The earlier replay-protection record and later peer-trigger memory shared CM-0010; the validator now scans both roots.
claim_states.inferred: Retaining the earlier assignment and migrating the later claim is the least destructive deterministic repair.
claim_states.proposed: Add aggregate namespace allocation or reservation before future continuity writes.
claim_states.superseded: CM-0010 as the identifier of the peer-trigger claim is superseded by CM-0013.
claim_states.unresolved: Full repository validator execution is not retained for this connector-only run.

next_action: Run the full continuity validator and, if clean for this collision, add an ID-allocation guard that checks every sanctioned storage root before record creation.
